fyPerformance: Q4 gross income grew 5.86% YoY to 1,515 Cr.
FY26 PBT grew to 882 Cr from 737 Cr in FY25.
Feed division FY26 sales stood at 562,060 MT versus 555,247 MT in FY25.
FY26 PBT margin stood at 15.5% of revenue versus 14.34% in FY25.
Shrimp exports grew 13.16% in rupee value and 6.9% by volume.

guidance.value: US and China remain key markets

highlights: Q4 feed sales stood at 123,725 MT.
Raw material costs increased for fish meal and soya bean meal.
US export volumes declined while China exports improved.
Frozen shrimp exports grew in rupee, dollar and volume terms.
Feed PBT was impacted sequentially by raw material cost inflation.
Company remains one of the major shrimp feed players in India.
Avanti Feeds Ltd.quote: "PBT during FY26 stood at 15.5% on revenue."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at 2,120 Cr despite tariff disruption in export markets.
FY26 EBITDA stood at 500 Cr and PAT at 338 Cr.
Q4 revenue grew 8.9% YoY to 597 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA grew 29% YoY to 157 Cr with margin at 26.2%.
Q4 PAT grew 39.1% YoY to 108 Cr.
highlights: Cash and liquid investments stood at 774 Cr at year-end.
Company remains debt-free.
More than 500 Cr has been deployed toward capacity expansion.
Sun control capex of 191 Cr was announced.
D2C margins are meaningfully higher than distributor margins.
PPF and sun control remain key growth drivers.

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue grew 4% to 4,065 Cr.
India operations grew 10% YoY despite US tariff uncertainty.
Q4 India operations grew 2% in a difficult tariff environment.
Company spent around 170 Cr toward new capacity creation.
Net debt increased mainly due to capex and working capital.
highlights: US and UK retail sales grew 8% and 6% in CY25.
Operating EBITDA margin in India improved to around 12%.
Subsidiary EBITDA margin stood around 5.9%.
Africa business margin is expected to improve in H2FY27.
Tariff normalization improves FY27 revenue and margin outlook.
Order flow remains good, but management is selective.

fyPerformance: FY26 was the highest-ever year for revenue, EBITDA and PAT.
Revenue crossed 1,000 Cr for the first time.
Q4 revenue grew 21% YoY to 243.3 Cr.
Q4 EBITDA grew 35.9% to 58.3 Cr with margin at 23.9%.
Q4 PAT grew 61% to 37.2 Cr.
highlights: Lab-grown diamond jewelry contributed 88.3% of Q4 export sales mix.
Online revenue accounted for 27.4% of Q4 revenue.
Cash and investments stood around 493.4 Cr.
ORIGEM Q4 revenue stood at 5.56 Cr.
April ORIGEM sales crossed 3.5 Cr.
ORIGEM store capex is around 40-60 lakh per store.
Goldiam International Ltd.quote: "For the first time in Goldiam's history, our revenue crossed 1,000 Cr."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue was broadly similar to FY25 despite a tough environment.
Annual pillow manufacturing capacity doubled from 13 Mn to 31 Mn pillows.
Branded revenues stood at 792 Cr in FY26 with sequential growth.
New business revenue stood at 270 Cr, around USD30 Mn.
Non-US core business contributed around 30% of core revenue.
highlights: Manufacturing facilities operate at around 65% utilization.
Management expects 60-65% utilization across facilities in FY27.
Domestic products are available at nearly 2,000 touchpoints.
FY27 is expected to be a defining year.
Incremental FY27 revenue addition targeted around 1,300 Cr.
Branded revenue is expected to nearly double from FY26 levels.
Indo Count Industries Ltd.quote: "We are on track to deliver a record year in FY27."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue including other income grew to 11,023 Cr.

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue crossed 5,000 Cr for the first time.

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue declined 11.5% to 9,468 Cr due to tariffs, demand softness and disruption.
Q4 revenue grew 7.7% sequentially, showing stabilization.
Q4 EBITDA margin improved 313 bps QoQ to 10.8%.
Full-year free cash flow stood at 956 Cr.
Net debt reduced 52% YoY to 775 Cr.

guidance.value: Measured volume recovery and margin progression
Profitable growth and working capital discipline
